title: Zero-Emission Vehicle Infrastructure Initiatives Across New Brunswick
content: The Government of Canada is funding three projects in New Brunswick to expand zero-emission vehicle infrastructure. Through the Zero Emission Vehicle Infrastructure Program (ZEVIP), Irving Oil will receive $1.2 million to install 16 EV fast chargers in New Brunswick and Nova Scotia, while the Power Commission of Saint John will receive $190,000 to install 10 public EV chargers. Under the Smart Renewables and Electrification Pathways Program (SREPS), $1.5 million will support studies and community engagement for two full-service EV charging stations on Indigenous-owned land along the TransCanada Highway.
author: Analysts Account
